zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry de la Pomeroy於1416年出生在英國戴文貝里 波梅洛易時，他的父親Sir Edward De La Pomeroy38歲，母親Margaret Beville37歲。他於1430年與Alice Raleigh在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1481年7月7日在他的家鄉享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pomeroy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin; mainly Devon and Dorset): habitational name from any of various places in northeastern France named with Old French pommeroie, pommeraie ‘apple orchard’ (collective of pomme ‘apple’). The name was established in Ireland in 1573.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry de la Pomeroy was born in 1416, in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England, his father, Sir Edward De La Pomeroy, was 38 and his mother, Margaret Beville, was 37. He married Alice Raleigh in 1430, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 7 July 1481, in his hometown, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry de la Pomeroy est né en 1416, à Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward De La Pomeroy, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Margaret Beville, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Alice Raleigh en 1430, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 7 juillet 1481, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de la Pomeroyが1416年にイギリスデボンバーリー ポメロイで生まれた時，父Sir Edward De La Pomeroyは38歳，母Margaret Bevilleは37歳であった。1430年にイギリスデボンでAlice Raleighと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1481年7月7日に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de la Pomeroy(이)가 1416년 영국 데폰 베리 폼로이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edward De La Pomeroy(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Beville(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1430년 영국 데폰에서 Alice Raleigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1481년 7월 7일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry de la Pomeroy родился в 1416 г. в местности бирри помирои, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Edward De La Pomeroy было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Beville, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Raleigh в 1430 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 7 июля 1481 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.
